name: mockaccino
description: Mock generator for testing C code using C++ gmock classes.

commands.title: Mockaccino: add MCP server to Claude Code (.mcp.json).

mockaccino.treatLonelyPreprocIfAsActive.description: Treat lonely #if - #endif preprocessor directives as always active (useful for mocking conditionally compiled functions).

mockaccino.skipStaticFunctions.description: Do not process functions with static specifier.

mockaccino.skipExternFunctions.description: Do not process functions with extern specifier.

mockaccino.disableDoubleMocking.description: Do not mock files that already have _mock in their name.

mockaccino.skipFunctionsWithImplicitRetu.description: Do not process functions that have implicit (int) return type, as most of them turn out to just be function-like macros used in file scope.

mockaccino.additionalPreprocessorDirecti.default:
#define FUNC(rettype, memclass) rettype
#define FUNC_P2CONST(rettype, ptrclass, memclass) const rettype*
#define FUNC_P2VAR(rettype, ptrclass, memclass) rettype*
#define P2VAR(ptrtype, memclass, ptrclass) ptrtype*
#define P2CONST(ptrtype, memclass, ptrclass) const ptrtype* 
#define CONSTP2VAR(ptrtype, memclass, ptrclass) ptrtype *const
#define CONSTP2CONST(ptrtype, memclass, ptrclass) const ptrtype *const
#define P2FUNC(rettype, ptrclass, fctname) rettype (* fctname)
#define CONSTP2FUNC(rettype, ptrclass, fctname) rettype (*const fctname)
#define CONST(type, memclass) const type
#define VAR(vartype, memclass) vartype
#define STD_ON 1
#define STD_OFF 0
#define EM_FILENUM(mod_id, fidx)
mockaccino.additionalPreprocessorDirecti.description: Additional preprocessor directives (if/elif/else, define) that will be prepended to each parsed file.

mockaccino.copyright.default:
Copyright (c) $YEAR [INPUT FILE OWNER]. All rights reserved.

WARNING:
PLEASE REPLACE COPYRIGHT STATEMENT IN MOCKACCINO SETTINGS!
mockaccino.copyright.description: Copyright text that will be included in each file.

mockaccino.ignoredFunctionNames.description: Comma-separated list of function names that should be ignored while parsing.

mockaccino.outputPath.description: Path where mocks should be generated. ${workspaceFolder} variable can be used here. If empty generate in the same location as input.

mockaccino.mockSourceExtension.description: Extension for the generated C-wrapper source files (_mock and _stub). The C mock header stays .h and the C++ class mock stays .hpp.

mockaccino.cpp.enabled.description: Also generate gmock mock classes (a _mock.hpp) for C++ interfaces found in the file, alongside the C-function mocks. The mock command always does both; turn this off to skip the C++ class path.

mockaccino.cpp.onlyVirtualOrInterfaceCla.description: Only mock C++ classes that are interfaces — abstract (have a pure-virtual method) or whose name matches cpp.interfaceNamePatterns. Disable to mock any class that has at least one overridable virtual method.

mockaccino.cpp.flattenNamespaces.description: How namespaced C++ mock classes are laid out. On (default): one flat global-namespace class whose name encodes the scope, deriving from the fully-qualified base (app::Outer::Inner -> class app_Outer_Inner_Mock : public app::Outer::Inner). Off: mirror the source namespaces — the mock is wrapped in 'namespace app::... { }' with a base relative to it (class Outer_Inner_Mock : public Outer::Inner).

mockaccino.cpp.interfaceNamePatterns.description: Case-insensitive substrings that, when cpp.onlyVirtualOrInterfaceClasses is on, additionally select a class for mocking by its name (e.g. add 'interface' to also mock non-abstract classes whose name contains 'interface'). Empty by default — only abstract classes are mocked.

mockaccino.includeDirectories.description: Include directories passed to the clang parser backend as -I (unused by the default regex backend). ${workspaceFolder} can be used in each entry.

mockaccino.clangPath.description: Path to the clang executable used by the clang parser backend. If empty, 'clang' is looked up on PATH.

mockaccino.clangSystemHeaderPaths.description: System header directories passed to clang as -isystem (e.g. a custom libc/SDK). Leave empty to use clang's own matching/built-in headers. ${workspaceFolder} can be used in each entry.

mockaccino.clangExtraArgs.description: Extra arguments passed verbatim to clang (e.g. -nostdinc, -resource-dir=..., -target=..., -std=c11). Escape hatch for controlling the header search and target.

mockaccino.ai.inputMode.description: What the AI parser backend feeds the model: 'fullFile' sends the whole source (the model resolves types/context); 'declarations' (default) sends only the candidate function-declaration strings the regex preprocessor extracts (cheaper, mirrors the regex backend); 'declarationsWithContext' sends those declarations to extract plus the whole file as type context the model may read but must not extract from.

mockaccino.ai.enableClaudeCli.description: Allow the AI parser backend to use the 'claudeCli' model source (shells out to the local 'claude' CLI). Off by default — enable it to let the AI parser run the 'claude' CLI.

mockaccino.ai.preferredModelSource.description: Preferred model source for the AI parser backend. 'sampling' uses the calling MCP client's model (Copilot supports it; Claude Code does not); 'vscodeLm' uses the editor's model (Copilot); 'claudeCli' shells out to the 'claude' CLI. On failure it falls back to the other available sources, and the method actually used is reported.

mockaccino.ai.claudePath.description: Path to the 'claude' CLI used by the AI parser backend's 'claudeCli' model source. If empty, 'claude' is looked up on PATH.

mockaccino.ai.claudeArgs.description: Extra arguments passed to the 'claude' CLI for the 'claudeCli' model source (e.g. --model). The prompt is always sent on stdin with -p.

mockaccino.mcp.enabled.description: Run the Mockaccino MCP server so AI clients (Copilot agent mode, Claude Code) can generate mocks/stubs. Copilot discovers it automatically; for Claude Code run the 'add MCP server to Claude Code' command.

mockaccino.mcp.port.description: Localhost TCP port for the MCP server. 0 picks a free port each start (fine for Copilot, which is told the current port). Set a fixed port if you use Claude Code via .mcp.json so the URL stays stable across restarts.

mockaccino.mcp.enableClangBackend.description: Allow MCP clients to use the clang parser backend (off by default; regex is always available over MCP).

mockaccino.mcp.enableAiBackend.description: Allow MCP clients to use the AI parser backend (off by default; regex is always available over MCP).

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
